name: Eloking
description: A consumer-facing game-services marketplace that sells account boosting, duo/teammate sessions and one-off coaching for competitive PC titles (League of Legends, Valorant, CS:GO, etc.).

details.targetMarket: Competitive PC gamers who want quick rank gains, duo teammates, or one-off coaching sessions in games like League of Legends, Valorant and CS:GO.

items: Rank/ELO boosting (solo boost, win boosts, division climbs)
Duo / teammate sessions where a pro plays alongside the customer
Game-specific service pages and a price calculator
Refund and flexible cancellation policy (refunds available before start)
Order tracking / communication with assigned booster
Blog and help glossaries per game
Trust signals (Trustpilot presence, company registration in Latvia)

missingFeatures: No standard, transparent tiered pricing — prices are calculated per order via a calculator rather than clear per-hour or per-session packages, which makes comparison and budgeting harder for buyers.

No public, verifiable booster reputation/metrics (e.g., win rates, hours played, verified skill badges) exposed per booster — discovery and trust rely on aggregate reviews rather than granular seller performance data.
No explicit ban-protection or insurance policy: site promotes security but provides limited public guarantees against game bans or platform enforcement outcomes.
No subscription or progress-tracking product for ongoing coaching (no recurring coaching plans with measurable progress dashboards), limiting retention and LTV expansion.
No public API or B2B integration offering for partners (e.g., esports orgs, stream platforms, or game-platform integrations) — platform appears consumer-only.

Limited transparency in dispute/fulfilment responsibility — terms indicate Eloking mediates but places responsibility for fulfillment between customer and player, which can create trust friction for higher-value orders.

pricingPlans.name: Custom order
pricingPlans.note: Boosting and duo sessions are priced per-order using an on-site calculator (price depends on game, current rank, target rank, and extras).

pricingPlans.name: Coaching (one-off)
pricingPlans.note: Coaching sessions are sold per session; price varies by coach and game — no public standardized hourly menu visible.

pricingEvidence.label: League of Legends boosting prices (company blog)
pricingEvidence.claim: Eloking publishes example price guidance (e.g., per-division price estimates) and explains its on-site price calculator.

pricingEvidence.label: Homepage / order calculator
pricingEvidence.claim: Main site promotes a state-of-the-art boosting calculator used to compute order prices dynamically.

companyEvidence.label: Crunchbase company profile
companyEvidence.claim: Lists Eloking (Eloking SIA) as a private company based in Riga, Latvia and shows headcount range.

companyEvidence.label: About page (company info)
companyEvidence.claim: Company describes founding story, lists registration in Riga and team background; positions itself as an official Latvian-registered company.

companyEvidence.label: Press / Accesswire (orders claim)
companyEvidence.claim: Accesswire reports Eloking completed over 4,000 orders in its first year (company PR).

reviewEvidence.label: Trustpilot reviews
reviewEvidence.claim: Eloking has an active Trustpilot profile with many positive reviews, used as a primary trust signal on the site.

reviewEvidence.label: Reddit user complaints
reviewEvidence.claim: Reddit threads document multi-month delays and poor booster communication for some customers, evidence of operational complaints.

reviewEvidence.label: Product Hunt / user review
reviewEvidence.claim: Independent review that describes a prolonged, poor-quality boost experience (long completion times, multiple boosters).

featureEvidence.label: What is Eloking (glossary)
featureEvidence.claim: Site documents core product: boosting, coaching, and a teammate/duo offering, plus booster screening.

featureEvidence.label: Contact / refund policy page
featureEvidence.claim: Contact page and support messaging describe flexible refunds prior to order start and expected booster start times; used to support claims about support and refund policy.

strengths: Narrow, clear product focus (boosting + coaching + duo) that simplifies onboarding for customers who only want rank or quick duet play.
Strong trust signals for a gray-market service: Latvian company registration, active Trustpilot presence and public customer-facing copy on refund and screening policies.
Operational features like a per-order price calculator and live-chat support reduce friction for one-off purchases and price discovery.
Competitive pricing positioning and frequent discount codes (company claims), which helps customer acquisition in a price-sensitive market.
weaknesses: Boosting is a high-trust/gray-market activity — despite vetting claims, third-party complaints (Reddit/Product Hunt) show occasional long delays and communication failures that can damage reputation quickly.
No clear ban-protection or formal insurance product; customers have limited public guarantees against account sanctions, increasing perceived risk for high-value orders.
Pricing is per-order and opaque for new buyers — lack of standardized hourly/session pricing or subscription coaching products limits predictable spend and repeat purchase models.

Terms and platform language indicate responsibility and dispute complexity between 'customer' and 'player', which can create legal/fulfillment ambiguity for buyers and increase refund/chargeback risk.
